content.claim: The UN Special Rapporteur on the right to education warned in March 2025 that church-school growth (now ~20% of schools, over 33% of secondary schools) is linked to resourcing gaps and 'hollowing out' of public education, with risks of increased segregation and disadvantage concentration in remote/rural state schools; full report pending to UN Human Rights Council June 2026.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: Assessment is independent human-rights monitoring, not Hungarian domestic law or legal authority
Full report not yet finalized; preliminary characterizations may be subject to revision
Causal link between church-school growth and rural public-school disinvestment inferred rather than quantified
Segregation and resource-allocation effects not independently verified by Hungarian authorities in sources provided
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
